summary:
Hydroxyproline is unique because it forms exclusively within the context of collagen biosynthesis and serves as a reliable marker for estimating total collagen levels due to its specific origin and structural role in stable collagen fibrils.

The answer is A — hydroxyproline.

We are asked about the amino acid used in the estimation of collagen; this refers not just any amino acid but one with specificity tied directly to collagen metabolism—hydroxyproline stands out precisely because it arises only after post-translational modification of proline inside collagens under enzymatic control involving vitamin C-dependent pathways. This makes hydroxyproline both structurally essential and quantitatively reflective—a direct proxy when measuring overall tissue or serum collagen quantity via biochemical assays such as those using urine tests where urinary excretion correlates well with daily turnover rate of skin/organ-based extracellular matrix components rich in type I fibrous proteins like dermal connective tissues.

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{A}}$
